message:
feat(frankenpandas): expose 15 window/accessor/groupby types (fd90.259 / br-zcwd0)

Major user-facing return types were unreachable from frankenpandas:

Series-level windows (return types of series.rolling/expanding/ewm/resample):
- Rolling, Expanding, Ewm, Resample

DataFrame-level windows (return types of df.rolling/etc.):
- DataFrameRolling, DataFrameExpanding, DataFrameEwm, DataFrameResample

GroupBy types (return types of df.groupby / series.groupby /
gb.rolling / gb.resample):
- DataFrameGroupBy, SeriesGroupBy, GroupByRolling, GroupByResample

Accessors (return types of series.str / series.dt / sparse access):
- StringAccessor, DatetimeAccessor, SparseAccessor

User code:
    let r: DataFrameRolling = df.rolling(10, None);

could not type-annotate the binding because the type wasn't reachable.

Same hidden-return-type pattern as fd90.257 (JoinedSeries) and
fd90.258 (DataFrameDictResult).

Fix:
- Add 15 types to top-level fp_frame re-export
- Add to prelude module
- Extend prelude_completeness_compile_guard with fn-pointer references
  using the lifetime-elided form (e.g. fn(&Rolling<'_>))

Verified: 36 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add pandas-oracle-backed packet fixtures for basic largest/smallest, keep semantics, and null ordering so top/bottom-N behavior is frozen against pandas.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T02:23:01.235398129Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T02:36:22.903964303Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T02:36:22.902223528Z","close_reason":"Completed DataFrame nlargest/nsmallest conformance packet coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","dataframe","sorting"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-zbz5","title":"[LOW] docs: README IO format table undercounts Arrow IPC stream as separate format","description":"Reality-check audit finding (post-/mock-code-finder + /reality-check-for-project).

**Observation.** README.md:137-147 declares "7 IO formats" and the IO Format Support table lists 7 rows: CSV, JSON, JSONL, Parquet, Excel, Feather, SQL. The Feather row's Options column says "Arrow IPC file + stream formats" — conflating Feather with the dedicated IPC stream wire format.

fp-io actually exposes **8 distinct IO format entrypoints**:
- read_csv_str / write_csv_string
- read_json_str / write_json_string
- read_jsonl_str / write_jsonl_string
- read_parquet_bytes / write_parquet_bytes
- read_excel_bytes / write_excel_bytes
- read_feather_bytes / write_feather_bytes  (Arrow IPC file format)
- read_ipc_stream_bytes / write_ipc_stream_bytes  (Arrow IPC stream, distinct wire format)
- read_sql / write_sql

The umbrella frankenpandas crate re-exports read_ipc_stream_bytes / write_ipc_stream_bytes as first-class fns (crates/frankenpandas/src/lib.rs:76, 94). They're not variants of Feather; they serve a separate use case (streaming pipes, zero-copy interchange) that Arrow IPC stream is designed for.

Also: the badge line declares "IO_formats=7" but the same badge should read 8.

**Severity.** LOW — documentation / metadata gap; no correctness impact. Users hitting the ipc_stream path will find the fns in rustdoc but won't see them in the README capability pitch.

**Fix shape.**

1. Split the Feather row into two rows or add an 8th row:
   | Arrow IPC stream | read_ipc_stream_bytes | write_ipc_stream_bytes | Yes | Yes | Streaming wire format, distinct from Feather file |
2. Update the badge in README.md line 13 from "IO_formats-7" to "IO_formats-8".
3. CHANGELOG.md Commit Statistics row "IO formats | 7" → 8.

Blast radius: README + CHANGELOG text edits only. No code, no fixture, no CI changes.

**Related.**
- br-frankenpandas-60du (closed): CHANGELOG stats regen. Next CHANGELOG refresh should bump the IO-formats row to 8.
- COVERAGE_MATRIX.md (br-zk1j) doesn't track IO fns by format so this gap is README-only.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:11:07.064359820Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:11:44.351531951Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:11:44.348941668Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","io","reality-check","review"]}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-zcwd0","title":"fd90.259: Expose window/accessor/groupby types (Rolling/StringAccessor/DataFrameGroupBy/etc.)","description":"fd90.259: Expose Rolling/Expanding/Ewm/Resample + DataFrame variants + GroupBy types + StringAccessor/DatetimeAccessor/SparseAccessor.

Major accessor + window types are unreachable from frankenpandas:

Series-level windows:
- Rolling, Expanding, Ewm, Resample (lines 6975-8239)

DataFrame-level windows:
- DataFrameRolling, DataFrameExpanding, DataFrameEwm, DataFrameResample (8431-8820)

GroupBy:
- DataFrameGroupBy (26754), SeriesGroupBy (8969)
- GroupByRolling (29491), GroupByResample (29612)

Accessors:
- StringAccessor (9730), DatetimeAccessor (11591), SparseAccessor (9664)

User code that wants to type-annotate any of these (e.g., 
`let r: DataFrameRolling = df.rolling(10, None);`) cannot reach the type names from the prelude.

Scope:
- Add the most user-facing ones (the 13 listed above) to top-level fp_frame re-export and prelude

Files: crates/frankenpandas/src/lib.rs.
Time estimate: 20-25min.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T07:34:19.650120833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T07:36:44.489693560Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T07:36:44.487235285Z","close_reason":"Exposed 15 fp-frame return types at top-level + prelude. Series-level windows: Rolling, Expanding, Ewm, Resample. DataFrame-level windows: DataFrameRolling, DataFrameExpanding, DataFrameEwm, DataFrameResample. GroupBy: DataFrameGroupBy, SeriesGroupBy, GroupByRolling, GroupByResample. Accessors: StringAccessor, DatetimeAccessor, SparseAccessor. All 15 are return types of major user-facing methods (df.rolling(), df.groupby(), s.str(), s.dt(), etc.). User code wanting to type-annotate a binding (let r: DataFrameRolling = df.rolling(10, None)) could not reach the type names from prelude. Same hidden-return-type pattern as fd90.257 (JoinedSeries) and fd90.258 (DataFrameDictResult). Extended prelude_completeness_compile_guard with fn-pointer references to each. All 36 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Mirror the existing DataFrame where DataFrame-other coverage: operation plumbing, pandas oracle dispatch, strict/hardened fixtures, and a focused packet test.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T06:32:18.011575878Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T06:40:16.984793984Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T06:40:16.983034652Z","close_reason":"Completed DataFrame mask DataFrame-other conformance packet coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","parity"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-zgnmz","title":"fd90.163: add Random Sampling integration test (sample + sample_weights)","description":"fd90.74-162 follow-on. The README's 'Random Sampling' section (lines 1626-1645) wasn't covered by integration tests. fd90.115 fixed the signature mismatches and fd90.162 fixed the compute_weight placeholder. Now lock in the corrected example via an integration test.

Fix: add 'readme_random_sampling_compiles_and_runs' which mirrors the README example. Construct a 100-row test DataFrame and verify:
  - df.sample(Some(n), None, false, Some(seed)) for n=100 rows
  - df.sample(None, Some(0.1), false, Some(seed)) for fraction
  - df.sample(Some(n), None, true, Some(seed)) with replacement
  - df.sample_weights(n, &[f64], replace, Some(seed)) with positional weights

Verify with cargo test -p frankenpandas. Pure additive test.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"test","created_at":"2026-04-26T16:02:59.907070396Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T16:05:05.424555940Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T16:05:05.421897762Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-zgqj","title":"[REALITY-CHECK MEDIUM] fp-conformance bootstrap_cases at 1212/37 with 8 cascading aggregates still failing 24h post lib-update sweep","description":"Reality: rch exec cargo test --workspace returns 351 fp-conformance / 360 fp-frame / 278 fp-io passing but fp-conformance has 8 cascading top-level aggregate failures (ci_gate_g6, ci_pipeline, e2e_suite, differential_*, packet_suite_is_green_for_bootstrap_cases, failure_forensics, grouped_reports, differential_all_packets_green) all rooted in bootstrap_cases at 1212 passed / 37 failed at the fixture level. README markets this as a working differential test framework with 20+ packet suites. Actual fixture count: 1249 fixtures across 430 packets. Production-shipped state has 37 fixture mismatches that no agent has burned down despite repeated tick activity since the 2026-04-22 lib-update sweep concluded. The README capability table claim Conformance testing against pandas oracle Yes (20+ packet suites) understates actual scope (430+ packets) but oversells current health (37 unfixed mismatches blocking 8 top-level gates). Suggested: track per-failing-fixture beads (subset by case_id pattern: dataframe_resample_*_with_nulls, series_dt_*, dataframe_pct_change/skew/round/value_counts/diff/abs/astype/memory_usage *_with_nulls, str_repeat_n / str_sub gaps) and burn them down in batches; revise README capability claim to actual 1212/1249 case-level pass count until 100% green.","notes":"cc-pandas triage 2026-04-22 (commit f0dcd9f): resolved on both axes. (a) Scale underclaim — README '20+ packet suites' updated to '430+ packet suites, 1249 fixtures, all green' (verified count: 430 unique fp_p2c_* packet IDs across 1249 JSON fixtures in crates/fp-conformance/fixtures/packets/). (b) Health overclaim — bootstrap_cases 1212/37 is gone: cargo test -p fp-conformance --lib under rch returns 387 passed / 0 failed / 0 ignored; full cargo test -p fp-conformance (lib + 6 integration suites) has every suite green with zero failures. packet_suite_is_green_for_bootstrap_cases AND differential_all_packets_green both pass. The 8 cascading aggregates (ci_gate_g6, ci_pipeline, e2e_suite, differential_*, packet_suite_is_green_for_bootstrap_cases, failure_forensics, grouped_reports, differential_all_packets_green) are all green. Fixture burn-down happened via parallel tick activity rather than a single sweep. Prose detail at README.md:856 also expanded the coverage list (added resample, rolling, groupby rolling/resample, datetime accessors, string accessors) to match what has actually landed in the packet corpus. No follow-up epic needed.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:24:09.046910504Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:16:44.549207299Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T12:16:44.546729742Z","closed_by_session":"cc-pandas-zgqj","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
